title: Imperial escort carrier (storm commando)
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Otheruses|Imperial escort carrier}} {{StarshipClass |image=[[File:Imperial_Escort_Carrier01.jpg]] |name=Imperial escort carrier |designer= |manufacturer= |line= |model=Imperial escort carrier |type=Escort [[Carrier/Legends|carrier]]''[[Star Wars: Rogue Squadron III: Rebel Strike: The Official Nintendo Player's Guide]]'' |cost= |modifier= |sysmods= |length= |width= |height= |mass= |max accel= |mglt= |max speed= |maneuverability= |engine=3''[[Star Wars: Rogue Squadron III: Rebel Strike]]'' |hyperdrive= |hdrange= |hdsystem=[[Hyperdrive/Legends|Equipped]] |poweroutput= |power= |shield gen= |hull= |sensor= |target= |navigation= |avionics= |maincomp= |countermeasures= |armament=*Single-barreled [[Turret/Legends|turrets]] (2) *Double-barreled [[Cannon/Legends|cannon]] (2) *Single-barreled [[Laser cannon/Legends|laser]] turrets (2) |complement=*[[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE/LN starfighters]] *[[TIE/sa tactical bomber/Legends|TIE/sa bomber]] *[[TIE/hu "Hunter" Multi-Role Starfighter|TIE Hunters]] *[[Escape pod/Legends|Escape pods]] (4) |bays= |escapepods= |crew= |skeleton= |passengers=*[[Storm commando/Legends|Storm commandos]] *[[Stormtrooper/Legends|Stormtroopers]] |capacity= |cargohandling= |consumables= |lifesupport= |communications= |othersystems= |availability= |role=*Medium assault craft *Carrier |firstuse= |retired= |affiliation=*[[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] **[[Stormtrooper Corps/Legends|Stormtrooper Corps]] ***Storm commandos }} {{Quote|Imperial escort carriers!''"
"''Imperial ''what''?"
"They're not part of the regular Imperial Navy. They're exclusive to the Imperial storm commandos.|[[Crix Madine/Legends|Crix Madine]] and Wedge Antilles upon seeing an escort carrier|}} The '''Imperial escort carrier''' was a type of [[Capital ship/Legends|capital ship]] used exclusively by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire's]] [[Storm commando/Legends|storm commandos]]. ==Characteristics== [[File:Imperial_Escort_Carrier02.jpg|thumb|left|200px|An [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] escort [[Carrier/Legends|carrier]] over [[Fondor/Legends|Fondor]].]] The [[Storm commando/Legends|Storm commandos']] Imperial [[Ton-Falk-class escort carrier|escort carrier]] was different from the [[Ton-Falk-class escort carrier|standard escort carrier]] of the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]]. It was a nimble, narrow-hulled ship, with a flared arrowhead bow, and three sublight [[Engine/Legends|thrusters]] mounted in a vertical row on the stern. Its most prominent weapons were two single-barreled cannon mounted turrets on the dorsal centerline, with a wide field of fire ahead, above, and to either flank. There were also two double-barreled weapons mountings on the ventral centerline, both fore of the [[Hangar/Legends|hangar]] bay, while two small turreted cannons were mounted on the very tip of the bow. They were slower than [[T-65B X-wing starfighter/Legends|T-65B X-wing starfighters]]. Like normal carriers, it was capable of deploying [[TIE series/Legends|TIE]] [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]] into battle, including the advanced [[TIE/hu "Hunter" Multi-Role Starfighter|TIE Hunter]], and it had a large hangar opening on each side of the hull, but it was also capable of engaging in combat on its own account. Ships of this type were used to attack medium-sized craft and [[Transport/Legends|transports]] of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]], boarding and seizing the Rebel [[Starship/Legends|starships]] and their crew. As the storm commandos were regarded as elite infantry, these ships were capable of deploying troops for planetary assault. The warship carried a complement of four [[Escape pod/Legends|escape pods]] in case of an emergency, with their designs being reminiscent of the [[class-6 escape pod]]. ==History== Following the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]] in [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]],''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' storm commando [[Officer/Legends|officer]] [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Sarkli]] flew a modified escort carrier with an improved [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]], in which he abducted scientists during the [[Raid at Bakura]]. He later fled to [[Geonosis/Legends|Geonosis]], where [[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge Antilles]] shot his craft down, starting the [[Battle of Geonosis (Galactic Civil War)|Second Battle of Geonosis]] of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. Sarkli's ship had personnel who wore [[Stormtrooper armor/Legends|stormtrooper armor]]. During the latter event, he was also accompanied by two other Imperial escort carriers, which eventually ended up destroyed by Antilles via the [[Void-7 seismic charge/Legends|Void-7 seismic charges]] included in his retrieved [[Delta-7 Aethersprite-class light interceptor/Legends|''Delta-7 Aethersprite''-class light interceptor]]. Imperial escort carriers were known to have guarded the [[Fondor/Legends|Fondor]] [[Fondor Shipyards/Legends|construction yards]], and fought during the [[Fondor shipyard assault|Battle of Fondor]]. ==Behind the scenes== [[File:Imperial_Escort_Carrier_Concept.jpg|thumb|left|180px|Concept art for the Imperial escort carrier.]] Concept art for the Imperial escort carrier depicts a brighter vehicle, with T-shaped wings that tie it more to the TIE line. It is also more lightly armed, more streamlined, and lacks a hangar bay. The original concept art emphasizes the speed of the craft and atmospheric capability, suggesting the original design could have been a transport rather than a [[Capital ship/Legends|capital ship]]. The Imperial escort carriers being exclusive to the storm commandos is ambiguous, as ''[[Star Wars: Rogue Squadron III: Rebel Strike]]'' depicts soldiers in [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtrooper]] armor as part of Captain Sarkli's ship's complement. These troops may have simply been storm commandos in stormtrooper armor, or the Imperial escort carriers may have carried a unit of Storm Commandos, as well as standard Stormtrooper elements. The Imperial escort carrier bears a name very similar to that of the escort carrier featured in [[Star Wars: TIE Fighter (video game)|''Star Wars: TIE Fighter'']] and its sequels, identified in the ''[[Stay on Target]]'' roleplaying game sourceboook as the [[Ton-Falk-class escort carrier|''Ton-Falk''-class]]. Wedge Antilles's unfamiliarity with the name used by General Madine for the starship suggests that neither the Storm Commando vessel seen in ''Rebel Strike'' nor the standard [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]] [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] transport seen in ''TIE Fighter'' were generally deployed (at least in operations Antilles might have been familiar with) prior to the Raid on Bakura. ==Appearances== *''[[Star Wars: Rogue Squadron III: Rebel Strike]]'' ==Sources== *''[[Star Wars: Rogue Squadron III: Rebel Strike: The Official Nintendo Player's Guide]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Imperial escort carriers}} {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Imperiales Geleitschiff |es=Portanaves de escolta Imperial/Leyendas }} [[Category:Carrier classes]] [[Category:Starship classes of the Galactic Empire]]
